# Obie Studios

> Obie Studios is an independent Android app studio that builds precision tools for developers and power users.

## About

Obie Studios was founded in 2026. Our focus is building Android applications that are genuinely useful — precise, polished, and respectful of the user. We do not build bloatware or ad-riddled software.

Developer website: https://obiestudios.com
Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/developer?id=Obie+Studios

---

## GPS Simulator: Mock Location

Our first app. A professional Android mock location tool.

**Play Store:** https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.gpssimulator
**Package:** com.gpssimulator

### What it does

GPS Simulator lets Android users set any GPS coordinates as their device's current location. It uses Android's built-in Mock Location API (via Developer Options) and does not require root access.

### Primary use cases

1. **Android developer testing** — Simulate any GPS location while building and testing location-aware apps, without physically traveling to that location.
2. **Privacy protection** — Broadcast a different location instead of your real one to apps that track location.
3. **GPS record management** — Record, replay, and export GPS routes for mileage logging, route documentation, and other record-keeping purposes.

### What it does NOT do

GPS Simulator does not bypass location anti-cheat systems in games. Apps like Pokémon GO detect mock location usage and may ban accounts. This app is not intended for, and will not work for, bypassing such detection.

### Key features

- Map-based location teleportation (tap anywhere on OpenStreetMap)
- Location search via Google Geocoder and Nominatim
- On-screen joystick with 5 speed tiers (Walk 1.5 m/s → Ludicrous 60 m/s)
- Route recording with adaptive filtering and OSRM road snapping
- Route playback with sub-step interpolation at 20 FPS
- Playlist system for chaining multiple routes
- GPX 1.1 import and export (Pro)
- GPS jitter simulation (0–100 m) and accuracy radius control
- Favorites system
- Offline support for local data management
- Translated into 80+ languages

### Pricing tiers

| Tier | Price | Features |
|------|-------|----------|
| FREE | Free | Basic spoofing, 1 favorite, ad-supported |
| NO ADS | Paid | Ad-free, increased favorites |
| EXPLORER | Paid | Joystick, recording, basic playback, road snapping |
| PRO | Paid | Full access: playlists, GPX, speed multipliers, unlimited favorites |
| Trial | Free | Full Pro features for 14 days, all new users |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Does GPS Simulator require root?**
No. It uses Android's standard Mock Location API in Developer Options.

**How do I set it as the mock location provider?**
Settings → About Phone → tap Build Number 7 times → Developer Options → Select Mock Location App → GPS Simulator.

**Is it free?**
Yes, with a free tier. A 14-day full Pro trial is available to all new users.

**Does it support GPX?**
Yes, GPX 1.1 import and export in the Pro tier.

**Does it work offline?**
Yes, for previously loaded maps and local data management. Internet required for new map tiles, location search, and road snapping.

**How many languages?**
80+ languages with full internationalization.
